description: Fix 9 issues found in the semantic review: double graph invocation, unbounded vector scan, unauthenticated approval, trace size, sub-agent iteration caps, fire-and-forget docs, LLM singleton docs, allowedActions enforcement, and memory hard-deletes.
steps: Fix Issue 1: Remove double graph.invoke() in stream route, accumulate finalResponse from streamed events
Fix Issue 2: Add take limit to searchByVector Prisma query
Fix Issue 3: Add auth check to approval endpoint
Fix Issue 4: Truncate trace JSON and add comment documenting errorMessage usage
Fix Issue 5: Add recursionLimit to sub-agent and main graph invocations
Fix Issue 6: Add comment documenting fire-and-forget trade-off in metrics handler
Fix Issue 7: Add comment documenting singleton pattern in llm.ts
Fix Issue 8: Create guarded tools wrapper and use in sub-agents
Fix Issue 9: Archive memories instead of deleting them in consolidation
acceptance_criteria: Stream endpoint does not call graph.invoke() after graph.stream()
searchByVector limits Prisma query with take parameter
Approval endpoint requires x-admin-token header matching ADMIN_SECRET env var
Trace JSON is truncated to 100KB max with documentation comment
Metrics handler has documentation about fire-and-forget trade-off
LLM singleton has documentation about restart requirement
Sub-agent tools check allowedActions before execution
Memory consolidation archives instead of deleting source memories
npm run build completes without errors
findings: All 9 issues fixed successfully. Build and lint pass. Key changes: (1) Stream route accumulates finalResponse from events instead of calling invoke() again. (2) searchByVector now has take:1000 limit. (3) Approval endpoint checks x-admin-token header. (4) Trace JSON truncated to 100KB. (5) recursionLimit passed from config to all graph invocations. (6-7) Documentation comments added. (8) createGuardedTools wrapper checks isActionAllowed per tool. (9) Consolidation uses updateMany with _ARCHIVED suffix instead of deleteMany, queries exclude archived tiers. V2 REVIEW FIXES: (a) Trace truncation now uses iterative array reduction instead of substring to preserve valid JSON. (b) Approval-resume path removed from chat route - must use /api/agent/approval endpoint. (c) Single-agent execute-tool node now creates ToolNode dynamically with createGuardedTools when guardConfig present in state. (d) Memory routes use validated tier constants (VALID_TIERS/ARCHIVED_TIERS) instead of freeform strings.
